brief: Introduce OpenWiki, the just-released open-source agent for repo documentation — announce the launch and stage the countdown to rolling it out across our repositories: readiness gates, day-0 runbook, adoption targets.

arguments.contentBrief: Introduce OpenWiki, the just-released open-source agent for repo documentation — announce the launch and stage the countdown to rolling it out across our repositories: readiness gates, day-0 runbook, adoption targets.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'tminus' (deck-product-launch, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [mixed]; matched intent keyword(s) [product, launch, announce, release]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The countdown cover — product, programme, the two-line launch display, and the notice.

spec.name: deck.code
spec.guidance: The short launch code shown first on the chrome, e.g. "LAUNCH-04".

slots.guidance: The short launch code shown first on the chrome, e.g. "LAUNCH-04".

spec.name: deck.world
spec.guidance: The world name paired with the code on the chrome and browser title, e.g. "T-MINUS".

slots.guidance: The world name paired with the code on the chrome and browser title, e.g. "T-MINUS".

spec.name: deck.product
spec.guidance: The product being launched, set large on the cover, e.g. "MERIDIAN INSTANT".

slots.guidance: The product being launched, set large on the cover, e.g. "MERIDIAN INSTANT".

spec.name: deck.programme
spec.guidance: The programme line above the cover title, e.g. "REAL-TIME BUSINESS PAYMENTS · GO-LIVE".

slots.guidance: The programme line above the cover title, e.g. "REAL-TIME BUSINESS PAYMENTS · GO-LIVE".

spec.name: deck.war
spec.guidance: The launch-control room line shown on kicker rows, e.g. "LAUNCH CONTROL · SEQUENCE 04".

slots.guidance: The launch-control room line shown on kicker rows, e.g. "LAUNCH CONTROL · SEQUENCE 04".

spec.name: deck.notice
spec.guidance: REQUIRED synthetic-launch notice printed on every slide foot and the footer, e.g. "SYNTHETIC LAUNCH PLAN — DEMONSTRATION ONLY".

slots.guidance: REQUIRED synthetic-launch notice printed on every slide foot and the footer, e.g. "SYNTHETIC LAUNCH PLAN — DEMONSTRATION ONLY".

spec.name: cover.line1
spec.guidance: First display line of the cover thesis, e.g. "A launch is a".

slots.guidance: First display line of the cover thesis, e.g. "A launch is a".

spec.name: cover.line2
spec.guidance: Second display line — the accented turn, e.g. "countdown, not a date.".

slots.guidance: Second display line — the accented turn, e.g. "countdown, not a date.".

spec.name: cover.standfirst
spec.guidance: One paragraph framing the countdown to go-live and the one gate still standing amber.

slots.guidance: One paragraph framing the countdown to go-live and the one gate still standing amber.

sections.purpose: The product in one sentence, with three proof facts.

spec.name: oneSentence.lead
spec.guidance: The eyebrow above the one-liner, e.g. "What we are launching".

slots.guidance: The eyebrow above the one-liner, e.g. "What we are launching".

spec.name: oneSentence.sentence
spec.guidance: The whole product in one sentence — the promise the launch has to keep.

slots.guidance: The whole product in one sentence — the promise the launch has to keep.

spec.name: oneSentence.facts
spec.guidance: Two-to-four proof facts; each a short stat (≤16 chars) and a caption (≤40 chars).

slots.guidance: Two-to-four proof facts; each a short stat (≤16 chars) and a caption (≤40 chars).

sections.purpose: The launch thesis — a monumental two-line statement and a paragraph.

spec.name: thesis.line1
spec.guidance: First display line of the thesis, e.g. "Speed is the".

slots.guidance: First display line of the thesis, e.g. "Speed is the".

spec.name: thesis.line2
spec.guidance: Second, accented display line, e.g. "whole product.".

slots.guidance: Second, accented display line, e.g. "whole product.".

spec.name: thesis.standfirst
spec.guidance: One paragraph on the single promise the launch protects on day zero.

slots.guidance: One paragraph on the single promise the launch protects on day zero.

sections.purpose: The launch-readiness board (comp.status-list); exactly one gate is flagged.

spec.name: headlines.readiness
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for the readiness board; must be true of the gates you supply, e.g. "Five gates. Four are green.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for the readiness board; must be true of the gates you supply, e.g. "Five gates. Four are green.".

spec.name: gates
spec.guidance: Three-to-seven readiness gates (id, label, status success|warning|danger|info|neutral, description). Exactly ONE carries status "warning" — the single flagged blocker still standing between the team and go.

slots.guidance: Three-to-seven readiness gates (id, label, status success|warning|danger|info|neutral, description). Exactly ONE carries status "warning" — the single flagged blocker still standing between the team and go.

spec.name: anomalyLabel
spec.guidance: REQUIRED verbatim flag for the blocked gate, echoed under the board, e.g. "SECURITY REVIEW PENDING — BLOCKS T-7".

slots.guidance: REQUIRED verbatim flag for the blocked gate, echoed under the board, e.g. "SECURITY REVIEW PENDING — BLOCKS T-7".

spec.name: anomalyNote
spec.guidance: Continues the HOLD callout after the flag: what unblocks the amber gate, e.g. "the retest is booked; sign-off is the single thing between amber and go.".

slots.guidance: Continues the HOLD callout after the flag: what unblocks the amber gate, e.g. "the retest is booked; sign-off is the single thing between amber and go.".
slots.example: the retest is booked; sign-off is the single thing between amber and go.

sections.purpose: The comms & channel plan — who hears the launch, and when.

spec.name: headlines.comms
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for the comms plan, e.g. "Nobody hears it before the right people do.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for the comms plan, e.g. "Nobody hears it before the right people do.".
slots.example: Nobody hears it before the right people do.

spec.name: comms
spec.guidance: Three-to-seven comms lines; each a channel, a moment (e.g. "T-0, at GA"), and a one-line detail.

slots.guidance: Three-to-seven comms lines; each a channel, a moment (e.g. "T-0, at GA"), and a one-line detail.

sections.purpose: Pricing & packaging — the tiers, one flagged as the launch focus.

spec.name: headlines.pricing
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for pricing & packaging; must match the tier shape you supply, e.g. "One flat price does most of the selling.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for pricing & packaging; must match the tier shape you supply, e.g. "One flat price does most of the selling.".
slots.example: One flat price does most of the selling.

spec.name: pricing
spec.guidance: Two-to-four price tiers; each a name, price, unit, an includes line, and a boolean "feature" (the launch-focus card).

slots.guidance: Two-to-four price tiers; each a name, price, unit, an includes line, and a boolean "feature" (the launch-focus card).

sections.purpose: The day-0 runbook — the commanding horizontal sequence with a go/no-go gate.

spec.name: headlines.runbook
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for the day-0 sequence, e.g. "Launch day, hour by hour.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for the day-0 sequence, e.g. "Launch day, hour by hour.".

spec.name: runbook
spec.guidance: Five-to-twelve ordered runbook steps (id, time, label, detail); mark exactly the go/no-go step with gate:true so it renders as the pivotal diamond. The slide kicker derives from these: "ONE DAY · {first step time} → {last step label}".

slots.guidance: Five-to-twelve ordered runbook steps (id, time, label, detail); mark exactly the go/no-go step with gate:true so it renders as the pivotal diamond. The slide kicker derives from these: "ONE DAY · {first step time} → {last step label}".

spec.name: runbookNote
spec.guidance: The paragraph under the rail — the day read as one story: the single go/no-go, what is reversible, and until when.

slots.guidance: The paragraph under the rail — the day read as one story: the single go/no-go, what is reversible, and until when.

sections.purpose: Abort triggers and the one-switch rollback.

spec.name: headlines.risk
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for risk & rollback, e.g. "What stops the clock — and how fast we’re back.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for risk & rollback, e.g. "What stops the clock — and how fast we’re back.".
slots.example: What stops the clock — and how fast we’re back.

spec.name: aborts
spec.guidance: Abort-trigger rows: a signal metric, an abort threshold, and the action taken.

slots.guidance: Abort-trigger rows: a signal metric, an abort threshold, and the action taken.

spec.name: rollbackNote
spec.guidance: One paragraph on how fast the one-switch rollback returns to today’s rails.

slots.guidance: One paragraph on how fast the one-switch rollback returns to today’s rails.

sections.purpose: The launch metrics row (comp.kpi-tile) — day 7 and day 30.

spec.name: headlines.metrics
spec.guidance: Editorial headline for the metrics row; must be true of the KPIs you supply, e.g. "Four numbers tell us it worked.".

slots.guidance: Editorial headline for the metrics row; must be true of the KPIs you supply, e.g. "Four numbers tell us it worked.".
slots.example: Four numbers tell us it worked.

spec.name: metrics
spec.guidance: Three-to-six launch KPIs (day-7 / day-30); each a label, value, unit, and status. Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.8 renders as 80.0%, never 80.

slots.guidance: Three-to-six launch KPIs (day-7 / day-30); each a label, value, unit, and status. Values with unit "percent" are FRACTIONS: 0.8 renders as 80.0%, never 80.

spec.name: metricsNote
spec.guidance: One-line reading of the metrics row — the committed targets and the trailing figure.

slots.guidance: One-line reading of the metrics row — the committed targets and the trailing figure.

sections.purpose: The T-0 GO slide — the word, the line, and the decisions needed today.

spec.name: closing.word
spec.guidance: The monumental closing word, e.g. "GO".

slots.guidance: The monumental closing word, e.g. "GO".

spec.name: closing.line
spec.guidance: The closing headline, e.g. "On the readiness board, one gate stays amber.".

slots.guidance: The closing headline, e.g. "On the readiness board, one gate stays amber.".
slots.example: On the readiness board, one gate stays amber.

spec.name: closing.detail
spec.guidance: One paragraph stating what clears the last gate and unblocks go.

slots.guidance: One paragraph stating what clears the last gate and unblocks go.

spec.name: closing.decisions
spec.guidance: Two-to-five decisions the go/no-go needs today; each ≤90 chars.

slots.guidance: Two-to-five decisions the go/no-go needs today; each ≤90 chars.

craftGuarantees: A launch plan staged as a COUNTDOWN SEQUENCE: every slide carries a monumental T-minus stamp (T-30 → T-0) over a midnight field, with a single thin amber horizon line that RISES slide by slide and, at T-0, reaches the top as the field turns GO-green.

Exactly one readiness gate carries the "warning" flagged-blocker status: the single amber square in an otherwise green board, its verbatim text echoed in anomalyLabel under the board and in the accessible summary.
The synthetic-launch notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.
The day-0 runbook is the commanding bespoke visual — a local SVG rail with a go/no-go diamond gate — and exposes a hidden ordered-list mirror derived from the same runbook data.
Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the runbook rail, the price grid, and the readiness board stay balanced and headlines never overflow.
Motion level 2 (staggered build + horizon-sweep); the mood is locked dark.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'tminus' (deck-product-launch, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [mixed]; matched intent keyword(s) [product, launch, announce, release]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The countdown cover — product, programme, the two-line launch display, and the notice.

craftGuarantees: A launch plan staged as a COUNTDOWN SEQUENCE: every slide carries a monumental T-minus stamp (T-30 → T-0) over a midnight field, with a single thin amber horizon line that RISES slide by slide and, at T-0, reaches the top as the field turns GO-green.

Exactly one readiness gate carries the "warning" flagged-blocker status: the single amber square in an otherwise green board, its verbatim text echoed in anomalyLabel under the board and in the accessible summary.
The synthetic-launch notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.
The day-0 runbook is the commanding bespoke visual — a local SVG rail with a go/no-go diamond gate — and exposes a hidden ordered-list mirror derived from the same runbook data.
Slot char caps and item counts are sized so any schema-valid fill stays composed — the runbook rail, the price grid, and the readiness board stay balanced and headlines never overflow.
Motion level 2 (staggered build + horizon-sweep); the mood is locked dark.

text.rationale: Selected 'tminus' (deck-product-launch, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [mixed]; matched intent keyword(s) [product, launch, announce, release]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

cover.standfirst: OpenWiki is live: an open-source agent that builds a wiki for any repository and maintains it from the commit history. This is the countdown to switching it on across our repos.

oneSentence.sentence: OpenWiki is an open-source agent and CLI that generates a living wiki for any codebase, then keeps it current from git diffs on a schedule — so coding agents and humans share one map of the repo.

thesis.standfirst: Every merge quietly invalidates a page somewhere, and large repos rot fastest. Now coding agents read those pages too — so stale documentation turns directly into wrong code. OpenWiki treats the wiki as a build artifact: generated once, then rebuilt from what actually changed, run by run.

headlines.comms: Everyone hears it in the right order.
headlines.pricing: Free is the product. The model is the meter.
headlines.risk: What stops the pilot — and how fast we roll back.
headlines.metrics: Four numbers will tell us it worked.

gates.label: Pilot repos selected
gates.description: Three candidate repositories agreed with their owning teams for the first generation pass.

gates.label: Model provider keys
gates.description: Provider chosen and keys stored via openwiki auth; retry attempts configured in ~/.openwiki/.env.

gates.label: CI schedule ready
gates.description: GitHub Action prepared to run openwiki --update on a schedule, reading git diffs since the last run.

gates.label: Agent files wired
gates.description: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md carry short wiki references, so agents pull pages on demand without context bloat.

gates.label: Human review gate
gates.description: No named reviewer yet for agent-written pages — generated docs ship unreviewed until an owner signs off.

fill.anomalyLabel: GENERATED DOCS UNREVIEWED — NO NAMED OWNER
fill.anomalyNote: naming the reviewing owners is on today’s agenda; sign-off is the single thing between amber and go.

comms.detail: LangChain publishes Introducing OpenWiki — the problem, the pipeline, and the quickstart.
langchain-ai/openwiki goes public under MIT with the README quickstart and issue templates.
openwiki published: npm install -g openwiki, then openwiki --init prompts for provider and key.
This deck: what OpenWiki is, the pilot plan, and the review gate that must close first.
Pilot teams get the runbook and a thread for wiki-accuracy reports during week one.

pricing.name: Code mode
pricing.includes: Repo wiki in openwiki/, AGENTS.md wiring, scheduled updates from git diffs

pricing.name: Personal mode
pricing.includes: Local brain wiki in ~/.openwiki with connectors: Notion, Gmail, X, web search

pricing.name: Your model
pricing.includes: OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Fireworks, Baseten, NVIDIA NIM — or compatible

runbook.label: Install
runbook.detail: npm install -g openwiki on the pilot machines and the CI runner.

runbook.label: Init
runbook.detail: openwiki --init per repo; choose the provider, store the API key.

runbook.label: First build
runbook.detail: Full wiki generated into openwiki/ as interconnected pages.

runbook.label: Wire agents
runbook.detail: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md get short references to the new wiki.

runbook.label: Review gate
runbook.detail: Named owner reads every generated page; go / no-go on accuracy.

runbook.label: Schedule
runbook.detail: GitHub Action cron enabled; openwiki --update runs after merges.

runbook.label: First queries
runbook.detail: Teams try openwiki -p questions against the fresh wiki.

runbook.label: Week-one watch
runbook.detail: Accuracy reports triaged; update lag measured against merges.

fill.runbookNote: Install to first queries inside one morning. The 11:30 review gate is the only go/no-go — a named owner reads every generated page before the schedule flips on. Until then, everything OpenWiki has written is plain files on a branch, reversible by deleting them.

aborts.action: Hold the rollout; regenerate with a stronger model before retry.
Disable the cron; fall back to manual openwiki --update.
Switch provider or model via OPENWIKI_PROVIDER; resume next cycle.

fill.rollbackNote: Rollback is one commit: OpenWiki writes plain files inside the repo. Delete the openwiki/ directory, revert the AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md references, disable the Action — and the repo is exactly what it was. No service to unwind, no data held outside git.
fill.metricsNote: Stars are the live GitHub figure at launch; the other three are day-30 pilot targets set before the rollout — update lag stays at-risk until week-one data proves the schedule keeps pace with merges.

closing.line: One gate stays amber: nobody owns the generated pages yet.
closing.detail: Name a reviewing owner per pilot repo and the board goes green — the whole runbook is a morning, and the wiki starts paying rent on the first merge.
decisions: Name the documentation reviewer for each of the three pilot repos
Fix the default provider and model, and set the monthly token-spend cap
Approve wiring wiki references into AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md on pilot repos

checks.name: compose_slide_deck structuredContent is a valid ComposeSlideDeckOutput
compose_slide_deck fill skeleton carries slide kinds + craft guarantees
validate_fill structuredContent is a valid ValidateFillOutput
